selected_need: AOF needs a Mission Control visibility layer that lets operators understand current mission state, artifact lineage, blockers, and recommended next action from one truthful surface.
intended_user_or_segment: Self-hosting AOF operators and AI sessions navigating the runtime after v3.5.0.
context_summary: The current visibility layer is useful but split across separate status, timeline, and flow outputs. Operators still reconstruct causal chains and next actions manually.
hypothesis: A bounded Mission Control layer built from canonical runtime artifacts will improve operator judgment speed without changing execution authority or inventing a second source of truth.
rejected_alternatives: Keep the current three-view surface and rely on operators to reconstruct lineage manually.
Jump directly to live pixel-office visualization before proving core operator value.
explicit_risks: Mission Control could drift from the underlying runtime if it stores new truth instead of deriving from canonical artifacts.
The UI could overclaim liveness if blocker and next-action data are not refreshed from runtime state.
delivery_validation_requirements: The first release slice must answer what is happening now, what created it, what is blocked, and what should happen next without raw JSON reading.
The graph and summaries must be derived from existing runtime artifacts or narrow new summaries, not handwritten dashboards.
need: Make AOF runtime visibility legible as a mission-level causal graph rather than separate status fragments.
intent: Ship a bounded Mission Control visibility slice before attempting richer live or ornamental views.
context: Post-v3.5 self-hosting runtime; command routing is improved, but operator situational awareness across artifact relationships is still expensive.
